title: Regime tagging engine (pulled forward via R1)
acceptance_criteria: Classifies each past trading day as RANGING | TRENDING_STRONG | VOLATILE_REVERTING | EVENT_DAY over reconstructed data (straddle_snapshots + index candles from T-56). Writes a per-day market_regime tag.

Migration 008 (idempotent): a market_regime-per-day table (date, symbol, regime, regime_confidence) AND a checked-in, dated event-calendar table (RBI policy / budget / F&O-expiry mornings) seeded with known dates.

POINT-IN-TIME / CAUSAL (Critical): the regime for day D uses ONLY data up to a fixed cutoff (prior close / intraday up-to-decision-time) — NEVER D's own close or any future day. A look-ahead audit test (mirroring T-56) asserts this: mutating future days must not change D's label.

DETERMINISTIC: classifier runs purely off injected/VirtualClock-fed inputs — no Date.now(), no wall-clock reads, no full-series statistics. Same inputs -> same labels (must hold under the 100x replay gate).
EVENT_DAY is sourced from the checked-in dated calendar table (migration 008) — NOT the live operator-supplied BLOCKED_DATES env var — so historical labels are reproducible and env-independent.
Documented precedence with deterministic tie-breaks at boundaries: EVENT_DAY > VOLATILE_REVERTING > TRENDING_STRONG > RANGING.
Fidelity-aware: reads T-55 resolution/gap tags; degraded (gap-marked / low-resolution) days are emitted as UNCLASSIFIED with a regime_confidence value rather than mislabeled.
schema.ts gains typed interfaces for the regime and event-calendar tables.
Unit tests cover: each of the four labels on representative inputs, the look-ahead audit, determinism (repeat-run identical), EVENT_DAY precedence, and UNCLASSIFIED on degraded input.
Named exports only; TS strict; passes tsc --noEmit and lint.
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
